payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields: (1) token_counts: input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens; (2) provider/model: provider_id, model_id; (3) estimated_price: USD cost computed from token counts and per-token rates; (4) latency_p50, latency_p90, latency_p95: response time percentiles in milliseconds. All fields must be recorded per inference to enable cost attribution and performance analysis.
citations: Token accounting: input/output/total token counts enable cost and latency per-token analysis
Provider/model IDs: required for pricing lookup and cross-vendor comparison
Estimated price formula: (input_tokens × input_rate + output_tokens × output_rate) / 1M
Latency percentiles: p50/p90/p95 capture tail behavior and SLA compliance
payload.decision: Document core accounting fields as durable memory with field definitions and measurement semantics

entity_resolution.reason: Standardize cost and latency measurement across eval runs

payload.intent: Explain required evaluation accounting fields for cost, latency, and token metrics in LLM observability

entities.name: input_tokens
entities.role: count of tokens in request prompt

entities.name: output_tokens
entities.role: count of tokens in response completion

entities.name: total_tokens
entities.role: sum of input and output tokens

memory_cards.statement: Token counts must include input_tokens, output_tokens, and total_tokens to enable cost and latency attribution
Provider and model identifiers must be recorded to enable cost lookup and performance comparison across vendors
Estimated price must be calculated as (input_tokens × input_rate + output_tokens × output_rate) / 1M tokens, using provider pricing at eval time
Latency percentiles p50, p90, p95 measure response time distribution in milliseconds from request submission to first/final token receipt

details: Documented 4 memory cards covering token counts, provider/model IDs, price estimation, and latency percentiles
Defined measurement semantics and relationships between fields
Provided formula for cost calculation and latency percentile ordering
Identified repair options for enhanced accounting (TTFT, caching, reconciliation)

repair_options: Add time_to_first_token field to distinguish TTFT from end-to-end latency
Include cache_hit_tokens if using prompt caching to adjust cost calculation
Record actual_price alongside estimated_price post-billing reconciliation
